before: Latin Jazz Quintet was one of the defining figures of Cool Jazz, whose recordings remain essential listening for anyone exploring the tradition.
after: The Latin Jazz Quintet was a Latin jazz ensemble led by conga player Juan Amalbert. It is best known for the 1960 album Caribe, recorded with Eric Dolphy and released on New Jazz in 1961, featuring Gene Casey (piano), Charlie Simons (vibraphone), Bill Ellington (bass), and Manny Ramos (drums and timbales). The group also recorded Latin Soul in 1965.
evidence: Wikipedia (Caribe) identifies a Latin jazz group led by Juan Amalbert; ours wrongly called them a Cool Jazz figure. Members and the Dolphy/New Jazz 1961 Caribe recording confirmed.
Live Wikipedia article: 'The Newest Sound Around is an album by singer Jeanne Lee and pianist Ran Blake', released 1962 on RCA Victor; the debut for both. Matches ours.artist.
